task: Extend Blue Life Commons with a public media intelligence platform surface that explains approved animal-image coverage, partner/source strategy, competitive lessons, and the operating loop for adding more animals and images safely.
surface: /media-intelligence route, public visual audit board, primary navigation, footer navigation, strategy documentation, and typed source/benchmark data.
audience: Researchers, NGOs, contributors, educators, reviewers, and partner media owners who need to understand how Blue Life scales animal images without weakening source, rights, or welfare standards.
first_read: Blue Life Commons is the approved visual layer for ocean life: every current animal has a rights-checked image, and the next job is scale through partners and source gates.

references.label: iNaturalist Seek
references.reason: Benchmarks community observations and image-recognition positioning.

references.label: GBIF Occurrence Image API
references.reason: Benchmarks occurrence image infrastructure and image-license cautions.

references.label: GBIF Multimedia Publishing
references.reason: Defines multimedia metadata fields and publishing constraints.

references.label: Encyclopedia of Life
references.reason: Benchmarks species knowledge and trait infrastructure.

references.label: OBIS Data Access
references.reason: Benchmarks marine occurrence data access and programmatic pathways.

references.label: Wild Me
references.reason: Benchmarks individual animal photo-identification and researcher collaboration.

references.label: Wildlife Insights
references.reason: Benchmarks bulk image intake, machine tagging, analysis, and public project workflows.

references.label: FishBase Hints
references.reason: Benchmarks fish media access routes and reuse caveats.

references.label: NOAA Fisheries Website Policies
references.reason: Anchors official-source image reuse and permission cautions.

keep: Current 31 of 31 approved species images remain visible as production proof.
Every public claim is source-backed or phrased as a strategy decision.
Competitors are treated as partner/source ecosystems, not enemies to copy.
Candidate and reviewer-only media remain blocked from public rendering.
avoid: Claiming to be the largest animal image platform.
Using Google Images as a source of record.
Promising photo identification without an implemented reviewed workflow.
Inferring image rights from biodiversity data availability.
direction.signature: A public media command surface: coverage now, visual ownership proof, benchmark lessons, source lanes, and the repeatable path from animal to live approved image.
system: Typed source and benchmark data in lib/media-intelligence.ts.
Server-rendered /media-intelligence route using live species/media counts.
Public visual audit board maps every approved image to its species page, guild, source host, rights status, license, asset id, and source page link.
No new external images; current approved species media is the Tier A proof layer.
Navigation routes users from Species to Media to Contribute.

asset_quality.why_premium: The primary asset is the real production media system: live approved species images and source-backed partner intelligence rendered as an inspectable product surface.

artifacts.notes: Public media intelligence route for coverage, visual audit board, benchmark lessons, source lanes, and expansion loop.
Typed benchmark/source/expansion data with source URLs.
Competitive/partner strategy, source priority, scale loop, guardrails, and best-platform answer.
Primary nav exposes the Media route.
Footer Explore column exposes the Media route.
SectionHeading now supports optional heading ids for route anchors and aria-labelledby wiring.
Full desktop route layout capture showing coverage, ledger section, partner benchmarks, source lanes, expansion loop, and footer.
Full mobile route capture for stacked layout and lazy-loading behavior.
Focused desktop visual audit board capture with decoded approved animal images and tracking metadata.
Focused mobile visual audit board capture with decoded approved animal images and readable tracking metadata.

checks.name: standards-loaded
checks.evidence: Repo instructions, Premium Web OS, asset intelligence, visual QA, and premium asset standards were read.

checks.name: source-policy
checks.evidence: The route and strategy cite official/source pages for iNaturalist, GBIF, EOL, OBIS, WoRMS, Wild Me, Wildlife Insights, FishBase, and NOAA.

checks.name: asset-tier
checks.evidence: Tier A product-proof surface: the page uses live species/media coverage from the production content system rather than decorative assets.

checks.name: export-inspection
checks.evidence: Production build served locally on port 3018 and inspected via desktop/mobile route screenshots plus focused ledger screenshots.

checks.name: visual-qa
checks.evidence: Desktop and mobile captures show no layout overlap; ledger cards render decoded animal images and readable metadata.

checks.name: local-build
checks.evidence: npm run lint and npm run build passed. Build still reports the known nonfatal Turbopack NFT tracing warning from app/sitemap.ts through lib/content.ts.

checks.name: public-safety
checks.evidence: The route renders approved media only through getApprovedSpeciesMedia; review-only candidate URLs remain excluded from public output.

score.notes: Strong production utility, live approved-image audit board, source-backed benchmark strategy, responsive QA, and build verification. Remaining score is reserved for future contributor submission/dashboard workflows.

known_risks: The live route must not overclaim platform size beyond the current corpus.
Future machine-assisted intake needs explicit reviewer gates before public use.
Additional animal pages still require source-backed species content and approved media records.
